rulings.Case Title: JOHN OWEN VS. CYNTHIA WALLACE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Deft Having Custody Of The Belongings Of The John Owen Decedent
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT CHERYL WALLACE NTC OF MTN AND MTN FOR DEFT HAVING CUSTODY OF THE BELONGINGS OF THE JOHN OWEN DECEDENT is DENIED. Moving papers are devoid of any legal authority supporting the relief requested. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FRANCES MARVEL ET AL VS. GABRIEL J. HOLMES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT ANDREW COLLINS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiffs failed to (1) attach the written contract to the Complaint or plead the contract's terms verbatim in the Complaint (2) plead sufficient facts supporting a cause of action for negligence (3) plead sufficient facts indicating a breach of habitability. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: FRANCES MARVEL ET AL VS. GABRIEL J. HOLMES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 7, 2024 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT FRANCES MARVEL, SIMON VILHELMSSON, THOMAS THELLY DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, OVERRULED in part; and MOOT in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for equitable indemnity is moot. Dismissal on file. Demurrer to the cause of action for contribution is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to identify a joint tort asserted/or could be asserted by the owner against the master tenant and subtenant for which both of them may potentially become judgement debtors at the end of this litigation arising out of the facts alleged in the Owner's Cross-Complaint. Demurrer to the contract-based causes of action for breach of written contract, duty to defend and duty to indemnify is OVERRULED. These causes of action arise out of an independent contract between Plaintiff and Bungalow Living, and not solely out of Plaintiff's Complaint/co-defendant's Cross-Complaint. E.g. see paragraph 20. Demurrer to the cause of action for negligent misrepresentation is OVERRULED. This cause of action is sufficiently stated. Blankenhelm v. E.F. Hutton (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 1463 is inapposite. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
